instruction: How do I use the TypeScript compiler API to parse a source file, walk the AST, and extract all function declarations with their parameter types?
output:
<think>
The TypeScript compiler API (exposed through the `typescript` package) provides full access to the parser, type checker, and AST. To extract function declarations with parameter types, I need to: create a Program or use ts.createSourceFile for parsing without type info, or ts.createProgram for full type checking. For type information, I need the type checker (program.getTypeChecker()). The AST walk uses ts.forEachChild for recursive traversal or ts.visitEachChild for transformation. Each node has a kind (ts.SyntaxKind) — I filter for FunctionDeclaration. For each function, I get its name, parameters (each has a name and optional type annotation), and return type. The type checker can resolve inferred types too via checker.getTypeAtLocation(). Key gotchas: ts.createSourceFile only parses (no type resolution), ts.createProgram needs a CompilerHost, and node positions are byte offsets not line numbers.
</think>

Using the TypeScript compiler API to parse and extract function signatures:

```typescript
import * as ts from 'typescript';
import * as path from 'path';

interface FunctionInfo {
  name: string;
  parameters: { name: string; type: string; optional: boolean }[];
  returnType: string;
  isAsync: boolean;
  isExported: boolean;
  line: number;
}

function extractFunctions(filePath: string): FunctionInfo[] {
  const program = ts.createProgram([filePath], {
    target: ts.ScriptTarget.ESNext,
    module: ts.ModuleKind.ESNext,
    strict: true,
  });

  const checker = program.getTypeChecker();
  const sourceFile = program.getSourceFile(filePath);
  if (!sourceFile) throw new Error(`Cannot find ${filePath}`);

  const functions: FunctionInfo[] = [];

  function visit(node: ts.Node) {
    if (ts.isFunctionDeclaration(node) && node.name) {
      functions.push(extractFunctionInfo(node, checker, sourceFile!));
    }

    // Also handle arrow functions assigned to const
    if (
      ts.isVariableStatement(node) &&
      node.declarationList.declarations.length === 1
    ) {
      const decl = node.declarationList.declarations[0];
      if (decl.initializer && ts.isArrowFunction(decl.initializer)) {
        const name = ts.isIdentifier(decl.name) ? decl.name.text : '<unnamed>';
        functions.push(
          extractArrowInfo(name, decl.initializer, node, checker, sourceFile!)
        );
      }
    }

    ts.forEachChild(node, visit);
  }

  visit(sourceFile);
  return functions;
}

function extractFunctionInfo(
  node: ts.FunctionDeclaration,
  checker: ts.TypeChecker,
  sourceFile: ts.SourceFile
): FunctionInfo {
  const symbol = checker.getSymbolAtLocation(node.name!);
  const type = checker.getTypeOfSymbolAtLocation(symbol!, node);
  const signatures = type.getCallSignatures();

  const params = node.parameters.map((p) => ({
    name: p.name.getText(sourceFile),
    type: checker.typeToString(
      checker.getTypeAtLocation(p),
      node,
      ts.TypeFormatFlags.NoTruncation
    ),
    optional: !!p.questionToken || !!p.initializer,
  }));

  const returnType = signatures.length > 0
    ? checker.typeToString(signatures[0].getReturnType())
    : 'unknown';

  const { line } = sourceFile.getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(node.getStart());

  return {
    name: node.name!.text,
    parameters: params,
    returnType,
    isAsync: !!node.modifiers?.some(m => m.kind === ts.SyntaxKind.AsyncKeyword),
    isExported: !!node.modifiers?.some(
      m => m.kind === ts.SyntaxKind.ExportKeyword
    ),
    line: line + 1,
  };
}

function extractArrowInfo(
  name: string,
  arrow: ts.ArrowFunction,
  stmt: ts.VariableStatement,
  checker: ts.TypeChecker,
  sourceFile: ts.SourceFile
): FunctionInfo {
  const params = arrow.parameters.map((p) => ({
    name: p.name.getText(sourceFile),
    type: checker.typeToString(checker.getTypeAtLocation(p)),
    optional: !!p.questionToken || !!p.initializer,
  }));

  const sig = checker.getSignatureFromDeclaration(arrow);
  const returnType = sig
    ? checker.typeToString(sig.getReturnType())
    : 'unknown';

  const { line } = sourceFile.getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(stmt.getStart());

  return {
    name,
    parameters: params,
    returnType,
    isAsync: !!arrow.modifiers?.some(m => m.kind === ts.SyntaxKind.AsyncKeyword),
    isExported: !!stmt.modifiers?.some(
      m => m.kind === ts.SyntaxKind.ExportKeyword
    ),
    line: line + 1,
  };
}

// Usage
const fns = extractFunctions('./src/api/handlers.ts');
for (const fn of fns) {
  console.log(`${fn.isExported ? 'export ' : ''}${fn.isAsync ? 'async ' : ''}function ${fn.name}(`);
  for (const p of fn.parameters) {
    console.log(`  ${p.name}${p.optional ? '?' : ''}: ${p.type}`);
  }
  console.log(`): ${fn.returnType}  // line ${fn.line}`);
}
```

instruction: Show me how to write a custom TypeScript transformer that automatically adds logging to all async function calls, transforming the AST during compilation.
output:
<think>
Custom transformers in TypeScript work at the AST level during the emit phase. A transformer is a function that takes a ts.TransformationContext and returns a function that transforms a SourceFile. The pattern is to use ts.visitEachChild to recursively walk the tree, and replace nodes you want to transform. For adding logging to async functions, I need to: identify async function/method/arrow declarations, wrap their body in a try/finally that logs entry and exit, and optionally log the time taken. I must use ts.factory to create new nodes (the old createXXX functions are deprecated). Key challenge: preserving source maps and not breaking the type checker — transformers run after type checking, so I only need to produce valid JavaScript AST. I'll show how to integrate the transformer with both the programmatic API and ts-patch/ttypescript for use with tsconfig.
</think>

Custom TypeScript transformer that injects logging into async functions:

```typescript
import * as ts from 'typescript';

// The transformer factory
function asyncLoggingTransformer(
  program: ts.Program
): ts.TransformerFactory<ts.SourceFile> {
  return (context: ts.TransformationContext) => {
    return (sourceFile: ts.SourceFile) => {
      function visitor(node: ts.Node): ts.Node {
        // Transform async function declarations
        if (
          ts.isFunctionDeclaration(node) &&
          node.modifiers?.some(m => m.kind === ts.SyntaxKind.AsyncKeyword) &&
          node.body &&
          node.name
        ) {
          return ts.factory.updateFunctionDeclaration(
            node,
            node.modifiers,
            node.asteriskToken,
            node.name,
            node.typeParameters,
            node.parameters,
            node.type,
            wrapBodyWithLogging(node.name.text, node.body, context)
          );
        }

        // Transform async method declarations
        if (
          ts.isMethodDeclaration(node) &&
          node.modifiers?.some(m => m.kind === ts.SyntaxKind.AsyncKeyword) &&
          node.body &&
          ts.isIdentifier(node.name)
        ) {
          return ts.factory.updateMethodDeclaration(
            node,
            node.modifiers,
            node.asteriskToken,
            node.name,
            node.questionToken,
            node.typeParameters,
            node.parameters,
            node.type,
            wrapBodyWithLogging(node.name.text, node.body, context)
          );
        }

        return ts.visitEachChild(node, visitor, context);
      }

      return ts.visitNode(sourceFile, visitor) as ts.SourceFile;
    };
  };
}

function wrapBodyWithLogging(
  fnName: string,
  body: ts.Block,
  context: ts.TransformationContext
): ts.Block {
  const f = ts.factory;

  // const __start = performance.now();
  const startDecl = f.createVariableStatement(
    undefined,
    f.createVariableDeclarationList(
      [f.createVariableDeclaration(
        '__start',
        undefined,
        undefined,
        f.createCallExpression(
          f.createPropertyAccessExpression(
            f.createIdentifier('performance'),
            'now'
          ),
          undefined,
          []
        )
      )],
      ts.NodeFlags.Const
    )
  );

  // console.log(`[ENTER] fnName`);
  const logEntry = f.createExpressionStatement(
    f.createCallExpression(
      f.createPropertyAccessExpression(
        f.createIdentifier('console'),
        'log'
      ),
      undefined,
      [f.createStringLiteral(`[ENTER] ${fnName}`)]
    )
  );

  // console.log(`[EXIT] fnName ${elapsed}ms`);
  const logExit = f.createExpressionStatement(
    f.createCallExpression(
      f.createPropertyAccessExpression(
        f.createIdentifier('console'),
        'log'
      ),
      undefined,
      [f.createTemplateExpression(
        f.createTemplateHead(`[EXIT] ${fnName} `),
        [f.createTemplateSpan(
          f.createCallExpression(
            f.createPropertyAccessExpression(
              f.createBinaryExpression(
                f.createCallExpression(
                  f.createPropertyAccessExpression(
                    f.createIdentifier('performance'),
                    'now'
                  ),
                  undefined,
                  []
                ),
                f.createToken(ts.SyntaxKind.MinusToken),
                f.createIdentifier('__start')
              ),
              'toFixed'
            ),
            undefined,
            [f.createNumericLiteral('2')]
          ),
          f.createTemplateTail('ms')
        )]
      )]
    )
  );

  // Wrap original body in try/finally
  const tryFinally = f.createTryStatement(
    f.createBlock(body.statements.slice(), true),
    undefined,
    f.createBlock([logExit], true)
  );

  return f.createBlock([startDecl, logEntry, tryFinally], true);
}

// Programmatic usage
function compileWithTransformer(fileNames: string[]) {
  const program = ts.createProgram(fileNames, {
    target: ts.ScriptTarget.ES2020,
    module: ts.ModuleKind.CommonJS,
    outDir: './dist',
  });

  const result = program.emit(undefined, undefined, undefined, false, {
    before: [asyncLoggingTransformer(program)],
  });

  const diagnostics = ts.getPreEmitDiagnostics(program).concat(result.diagnostics);
  for (const d of diagnostics) {
    const msg = ts.flattenDiagnosticMessageText(d.messageText, '\n');
    console.error(msg);
  }
}
```

instruction: How can I use the TypeScript Language Service API to build a custom code intelligence tool that provides hover information and go-to-definition for a custom DSL embedded in template literals?
output:
<think>
The TypeScript Language Service is what powers VS Code's TypeScript intellisense. You can create a custom LanguageServiceHost to provide virtual documents, and then use the LanguageService for completions, hover, go-to-definition, etc. For a custom DSL in template literals, the approach is: create a language service plugin that intercepts requests for positions inside tagged template literals, parses the DSL content, maps positions between the template and virtual documents, and provides custom completions/hover. The LanguageServiceHost interface requires implementing getScriptFileNames, getScriptVersion, getScriptSnapshot, getCompilationSettings, and getDefaultLibFileName. For the DSL part, I need to detect when the cursor is inside a specific tagged template (like sql`...` or gql`...`), extract the string content, and provide custom intelligence. This is how graphql-tag and similar tools work.
</think>

Building a custom Language Service plugin for DSL intelligence:

```typescript
import * as ts from 'typescript';

// Custom Language Service Host for in-memory files
class CustomServiceHost implements ts.LanguageServiceHost {
  private files = new Map<string, { content: string; version: number }>();

  constructor(private compilerOptions: ts.CompilerOptions) {}

  addFile(fileName: string, content: string) {
    const existing = this.files.get(fileName);
    this.files.set(fileName, {
      content,
      version: existing ? existing.version + 1 : 0,
    });
  }

  getScriptFileNames = () => [...this.files.keys()];
  getScriptVersion = (f: string) => String(this.files.get(f)?.version ?? 0);
  getCompilationSettings = () => this.compilerOptions;
  getCurrentDirectory = () => process.cwd();
  getDefaultLibFileName = (opts: ts.CompilerOptions) => ts.getDefaultLibFilePath(opts);

  getScriptSnapshot(fileName: string): ts.IScriptSnapshot | undefined {
    const file = this.files.get(fileName);
    if (file) return ts.ScriptSnapshot.fromString(file.content);
    // Fall back to disk for lib files
    try {
      const content = ts.sys.readFile(fileName);
      return content ? ts.ScriptSnapshot.fromString(content) : undefined;
    } catch {
      return undefined;
    }
  }
}

// DSL intelligence for sql`` tagged templates
interface DSLHoverInfo {
  text: string;
  span: { start: number; length: number };
}

class SQLDSLProvider {
  private tableSchema: Map<string, { column: string; type: string }[]>;

  constructor(schemaFile: string) {
    this.tableSchema = this.loadSchema(schemaFile);
  }

  getHoverInfo(sqlText: string, position: number): DSLHoverInfo | null {
    const word = this.getWordAt(sqlText, position);
    if (!word) return null;

    // Check if it's a known table
    const table = this.tableSchema.get(word.toLowerCase());
    if (table) {
      const cols = table.map(c => `  ${c.column}: ${c.type}`).join('\n');
      return {
        text: `**Table: ${word}**\n\nColumns:\n${cols}`,
        span: { start: position - word.length, length: word.length },
      };
    }

    // Check if it's a known column
    for (const [tableName, cols] of this.tableSchema) {
      const col = cols.find(c => c.column === word.toLowerCase());
      if (col) {
        return {
          text: `**Column: ${word}** (${tableName})\nType: ${col.type}`,
          span: { start: position - word.length, length: word.length },
        };
      }
    }

    return null;
  }

  private getWordAt(text: string, pos: number): string | null {
    const before = text.slice(0, pos).match(/[\w]+$/);
    const after = text.slice(pos).match(/^[\w]+/);
    return (before?.[0] ?? '') + (after?.[0] ?? '') || null;
  }

  private loadSchema(path: string): Map<string, { column: string; type: string }[]> {
    // In practice, parse a schema file or query information_schema
    return new Map([
      ['users', [
        { column: 'id', type: 'INTEGER PRIMARY KEY' },
        { column: 'email', type: 'TEXT NOT NULL' },
        { column: 'created_at', type: 'TIMESTAMP' },
      ]],
    ]);
  }
}

// Plugin that intercepts Language Service calls
function createPlugin(info: ts.server.PluginCreateInfo) {
  const ls = info.languageService;
  const dsl = new SQLDSLProvider('./schema.json');

  const proxy = Object.create(null) as ts.LanguageService;
  for (const k of Object.keys(ls) as (keyof ts.LanguageService)[]) {
    (proxy as any)[k] = (ls as any)[k];
  }

  // Override getQuickInfoAtPosition for hover
  proxy.getQuickInfoAtPosition = (fileName, position) => {
    const program = ls.getProgram()!;
    const source = program.getSourceFile(fileName);
    if (!source) return ls.getQuickInfoAtPosition(fileName, position);

    const node = findNodeAtPosition(source, position);
    const templateInfo = findEnclosingTaggedTemplate(node, 'sql');

    if (templateInfo) {
      const relativePos = position - templateInfo.contentStart;
      const hover = dsl.getHoverInfo(templateInfo.text, relativePos);
      if (hover) {
        return {
          kind: ts.ScriptElementKind.string,
          kindModifiers: '',
          textSpan: {
            start: templateInfo.contentStart + hover.span.start,
            length: hover.span.length,
          },
          displayParts: [{ kind: 'text', text: hover.text }],
        };
      }
    }

    return ls.getQuickInfoAtPosition(fileName, position);
  };

  return proxy;
}

function findNodeAtPosition(source: ts.SourceFile, pos: number): ts.Node {
  let found: ts.Node = source;
  function visit(node: ts.Node) {
    if (pos >= node.getStart() && pos < node.getEnd()) {
      found = node;
      ts.forEachChild(node, visit);
    }
  }
  visit(source);
  return found;
}

function findEnclosingTaggedTemplate(
  node: ts.Node,
  tagName: string
): { text: string; contentStart: number } | null {
  let current: ts.Node | undefined = node;
  while (current) {
    if (
      ts.isTaggedTemplateExpression(current) &&
      ts.isIdentifier(current.tag) &&
      current.tag.text === tagName
    ) {
      const template = current.template;
      if (ts.isNoSubstitutionTemplateLiteral(template)) {
        return {
          text: template.text,
          contentStart: template.getStart() + 1,
        };
      }
    }
    current = current.parent;
  }
  return null;
}

export = createPlugin;
```

instruction: How do I programmatically use the TypeScript compiler API to type-check code in memory, report diagnostics, and auto-fix certain categories of errors?
output:
<think>
Programmatic type checking without files on disk is useful for linters, code generation validation, and testing. The approach: create a CompilerHost that serves files from memory (Map<string, string>), create a Program, and call getPreEmitDiagnostics. For auto-fixing, TypeScript's code fix API (languageService.getCodeFixesAtPosition) provides suggested fixes that can be applied as text edits. The flow is: get diagnostics, filter for fixable error codes, get code fixes for each, apply the text changes. Key detail: some fixes require multiple text changes across files. I need to apply changes in reverse order (by position) so earlier changes don't shift later positions. For the in-memory host, I need to handle lib.d.ts resolution — either bundle the lib files or point to the installed TypeScript's lib directory. The CompilerHost needs fileExists, readFile, and getSourceFile at minimum.
</think>

In-memory type checking with auto-fix:

```typescript
import * as ts from 'typescript';
import * as path from 'path';

interface InMemoryFile {
  content: string;
  version: number;
}

class InMemoryCompiler {
  private files = new Map<string, InMemoryFile>();
  private options: ts.CompilerOptions;

  constructor(options: Partial<ts.CompilerOptions> = {}) {
    this.options = {
      target: ts.ScriptTarget.ES2020,
      module: ts.ModuleKind.ESNext,
      strict: true,
      noEmit: true,
      ...options,
    };
  }

  addFile(name: string, content: string) {
    const existing = this.files.get(name);
    this.files.set(name, {
      content,
      version: existing ? existing.version + 1 : 0,
    });
  }

  typeCheck(): ts.Diagnostic[] {
    const host = this.createCompilerHost();
    const fileNames = [...this.files.keys()];
    const program = ts.createProgram(fileNames, this.options, host);
    return [
      ...program.getSyntacticDiagnostics(),
      ...program.getSemanticDiagnostics(),
    ];
  }

  typeCheckAndFix(maxPasses = 3): { diagnostics: ts.Diagnostic[]; fixes: number } {
    let totalFixes = 0;

    for (let pass = 0; pass < maxPasses; pass++) {
      const host = this.createServiceHost();
      const service = ts.createLanguageService(host);
      const diagnostics = this.getAllDiagnostics(service);

      if (diagnostics.length === 0) break;

      let fixedThisPass = 0;
      for (const diag of diagnostics) {
        if (!diag.file || diag.start === undefined) continue;

        const fixes = service.getCodeFixesAtPosition(
          diag.file.fileName,
          diag.start,
          diag.start + (diag.length ?? 0),
          [diag.code],
          {},
          {}
        );

        if (fixes.length > 0) {
          this.applyFix(fixes[0]);
          fixedThisPass++;
        }
      }

      totalFixes += fixedThisPass;
      if (fixedThisPass === 0) break; // No more auto-fixable errors
    }

    return { diagnostics: this.typeCheck(), fixes: totalFixes };
  }

  private applyFix(fix: ts.CodeFixAction) {
    for (const change of fix.changes) {
      const file = this.files.get(change.fileName);
      if (!file) continue;

      // Apply text changes in reverse order
      const sorted = [...change.textChanges].sort(
        (a, b) => b.span.start - a.span.start
      );

      let content = file.content;
      for (const edit of sorted) {
        content =
          content.slice(0, edit.span.start) +
          edit.newText +
          content.slice(edit.span.start + edit.span.length);
      }

      this.addFile(change.fileName, content);
    }
  }

  getFileContent(name: string): string | undefined {
    return this.files.get(name)?.content;
  }

  formatDiagnostics(diagnostics: ts.Diagnostic[]): string {
    return ts.formatDiagnosticsWithColorAndContext(diagnostics, {
      getCurrentDirectory: () => '/',
      getCanonicalFileName: f => f,
      getNewLine: () => '\n',
    });
  }

  private getAllDiagnostics(service: ts.LanguageService): ts.Diagnostic[] {
    const all: ts.Diagnostic[] = [];
    for (const fileName of this.files.keys()) {
      all.push(
        ...service.getSyntacticDiagnostics(fileName),
        ...service.getSemanticDiagnostics(fileName)
      );
    }
    return all;
  }

  private createCompilerHost(): ts.CompilerHost {
    const defaultHost = ts.createCompilerHost(this.options);
    return {
      ...defaultHost,
      getSourceFile: (name, target) => {
        const file = this.files.get(name);
        if (file) {
          return ts.createSourceFile(name, file.content, target);
        }
        return defaultHost.getSourceFile(name, target);
      },
      fileExists: (name) =>
        this.files.has(name) || defaultHost.fileExists(name),
      readFile: (name) =>
        this.files.get(name)?.content ?? defaultHost.readFile(name),
    };
  }

  private createServiceHost(): ts.LanguageServiceHost {
    return {
      getScriptFileNames: () => [...this.files.keys()],
      getScriptVersion: (f) => String(this.files.get(f)?.version ?? 0),
      getScriptSnapshot: (f) => {
        const file = this.files.get(f);
        if (file) return ts.ScriptSnapshot.fromString(file.content);
        try {
          const content = ts.sys.readFile(f);
          return content ? ts.ScriptSnapshot.fromString(content) : undefined;
        } catch { return undefined; }
      },
      getCurrentDirectory: () => '/',
      getCompilationSettings: () => this.options,
      getDefaultLibFileName: ts.getDefaultLibFilePath,
      fileExists: (f) => this.files.has(f) || ts.sys.fileExists(f),
      readFile: (f) => this.files.get(f)?.content ?? ts.sys.readFile(f),
    };
  }
}

// Usage
const compiler = new InMemoryCompiler({ strict: true });
compiler.addFile('/app.ts', `
  const x: number = "hello";
  function greet(name) { return name; }
`);

const { diagnostics, fixes } = compiler.typeCheckAndFix();
console.log(`Applied ${fixes} fixes`);
console.log(compiler.formatDiagnostics(diagnostics));
console.log(compiler.getFileContent('/app.ts'));
```
